title: Fixture for testing system
abstract: A fixture for an automatic needle injection is disclosed, wherein the fixture comprises a housing including a chamber, the housing including a first plurality of walls, a second plurality of walls, and a third plurality of walls, a holder, and a membrane; wherein the membrane is contained in the holder, the holder is secured within a portion of the housing, and the holder is configured to allow a portion of a needle of a syringe to pass through a portion of the holder and into the chamber of the housing.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A fixture for an automatic needle injection system, the fixture comprising: a housing including a chamber, the housing including a first plurality of walls, a second plurality of walls, and a third plurality of walls, a holder, and a membrane; wherein the membrane is contained in the holder, the holder is secured within a portion of the housing, and the holder is configured to allow a portion of a needle of a syringe to pass through a portion of the holder and into the chamber of the housing. 2. The fixture of claim 1, wherein the fixture is configured to be secured to a bottom portion of the automatic needle injection system, wherein the syringe and fixture are coaxially aligned with the automatic needle injection system. 3. The fixture of claim 1, wherein the holder includes a first portion, a second portion, and a plurality of cutouts, and the membrane is secured in the holder between the first portion and the second portion. 4. The fixture of claim 3, wherein the first portion includes a first plurality of cutouts and the second portion includes a second plurality of cutouts, the first plurality of cutouts aligning with the second plurality of cutouts. 5. The fixture of claim 3, wherein the plurality of cutouts on the holder designate puncture sites for receiving the needle. 6. The fixture of claim 1, wherein the holder is configured to rotate within the housing. 7. The fixture of claim 1, wherein the membrane has a thickness ranging from about 0.1 mm to about 1.0 mm. 8. The fixture of claim 1, wherein the holder has a height ranging from about 1 mm to about 10 mm. 9. The fixture of claim 1 , further comprising a container for receiving a sample from the syringe. 10. The fixture of claim 1, wherein the holder is a cassette and the membrane is configured to be fed through the cassette. 11. A fixture for an automatic needle injection system, the fixture comprising: a housing including a chamber, a holder including a first portion, a second portion, and a plurality of cutouts, and a membrane, wherein the membrane is secured between the first portion and the second portion of the holder; wherein the holder is secured within a portion of the housing and the holder is configured to allow a portion of a needle of a syringe to pass through a portion of the holder and into the chamber of the housing. 12. The fixture of claim 11, wherein the holder is configured to rotate within the housing. 13. The fixture of claim 11, wherein the holder has a height ranging from about 1 mm to about 10 mm. 14. The fixture of claim 11, further comprising a container for receiving a sample from the syringe. 15. The fixture of claim 11, wherein the plurality 7 of cutouts on the holder designate puncture sites for receiving the needle. 16. A method for testing a syringe, the method comprising: securing a fixture to an automatic needle injection system, the fixture comprising: a housing including a chamber; a holder; and a membrane; wherein the membrane is contained in the holder, the holder is secured within a portion of the housing, and the holder is configured to allow a needle of the syringe to pass through the holder and into the chamber of the housing, inserting a syringe into the automatic needle injection system, and programing the automatic needle injection system such that a needle of the syringe punctures the membrane. 17. The method of claim 16, further comprising securing the fixture to a base portion of the test system. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the test system is adjusted such that an unused portion of the membrane is punctured for each test run. 19. The method of claim 1 , wherein the needle extends through a portion of the housing, a portion of the holder, and into the chamber. 20. The method of claim 16, wherein the membrane is punctured at least 10 times.
